version_history.subject: [PATCH v4 00/10] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 01/10] fuse: separate next request fetching from sending logic
series_items.patch_summary: This patch separates the logic of fetching and sending the next request in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) driver from io_uring, making the control flow clearer and reducing unnecessary parameter passing. The duplicated logic in fuse_uring_send_next_to_ring() is removed, and a new function fuse_uring_get_next_fuse_req() is introduced to fetch the next request without sending it immediately. This change simplifies the code and improves maintainability.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 02/10] fuse: refactor io-uring header copying to ring
series_items.patch_summary: This patch refactors the logic for copying headers to a ring in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) io-uring implementation. It extracts the header copying into a new function called copy_header_to_ring(), which simplifies the code and centralizes error handling and rate-limited logging. The changes affect several functions in fs/fuse/dev_uring.c, making it easier to maintain and modify the code.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 03/10] fuse: refactor io-uring header copying from ring
series_items.patch_summary: This patch refactors the logic for copying headers from a ring in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) io-uring implementation. It extracts this logic into a new function called copy_header_from_ring(), making it clearer and more centralized, and also improves error handling and logging.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 04/10] fuse: use enum types for header copying
series_items.patch_summary: This patch improves the interface for copying headers in the FUSE file system by introducing enum types to identify which part of the header needs to be copied. This simplifies both kernel-space and user-space header addresses copying, particularly when buffer rings are added. The patch introduces an enum type `fuse_uring_header_type` with values for different header types and modifies existing functions to use this new type. The changes improve code readability and maintainability.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 05/10] fuse: refactor setting up copy state for payload copying
series_items.patch_summary: This patch refactors the code for setting up copy state in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) driver, specifically for payload copying. A new helper function called `setup_fuse_copy_state` is introduced to encapsulate this logic, making the code more modular and easier to maintain. The function takes several parameters, including a pointer to the copy state structure, a fuse ring, a request, an entry in the ring, and an iterator. It initializes the copy state and imports user buffers as needed. This patch is part of a larger series of changes to improve the FUSE driver's performance and reliability.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 06/10] fuse: use named constants for io-uring iovec indices
series_items.patch_summary: This patch replaces magic indices 0 and 1 in the iovec array with named constants FUSE_URING_IOV_HEADERS and FUSE_URING_IOV_PAYLOAD, making the code self-documenting and preparing for future buffer ring support. The changes are made in the fuse:dev_uring.c file, specifically in the functions fuse_uring_get_iovec_from_sqe and fuse_uring_create_ring_ent. This patch is part of a larger series of 10 patches and has been reviewed by several kernel developers.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 07/10] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools (implemented as buffer ring)
series_items.patch_summary: This patch introduces a buffer pool feature to the fuse io-uring implementation, allowing the server to pass in a contiguous allocation of buffers that can be used on demand by the kernel for servicing requests. This decouples the number of buffers required from the number of entries in the ring, reducing memory usage and enabling future optimizations such as incremental buffer consumption. The patch adds support for a FUSE_URING_BUFPOOL flag during registration and introduces a buffer ring data structure to manage buffer selection and recycling. The server must provide the payload buffer pool through an iovec payload in the first sqe, and the kernel will select and recycle buffers from this pool as needed.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 08/10] fuse: support registered buffers as buffer pools in io-uring
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds support for registered buffers as buffer pools in io-uring, allowing servers to use an io-uring registered buffer as the buffer pool and eliminating the overhead of pinning/unpinning user pages per i/o request. The server must register the payload buffers through io-uring with the FUSE_URING_REGISTERED_BUFFERS init flag set, and follow standard protocol for using registered buffers. This patch also includes kernel support for registered buffers advertised to userspace via the FUSE_HAS_IO_URING_BUFPOOL init flag. Benchmarks show a 10% throughput improvement for direct randreads and other improvements for different types of reads.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 09/10] fuse: add zero-copy over io-uring
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds zero-copy support for fuse io-uring, allowing the kernel to eliminate memory copies between userspace, the kernel, and the fuse server for read/write operations. The server can directly access client pages or page cache folios without copying data through an intermediary buffer. This is achieved by registering relevant pages in a sparse slot of the server's io_uring registered buffer table, enabling the server to operate on these pages using fixed buffer operations. The patch requires CAP_SYS_ADMIN and FUSE_URING_REGISTERED_BUFFERS capabilities and introduces a new flag FUSE_HAS_IO_URING_BUFPOOL to advertise kernel support for zero copy to userspace.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 10/10] docs: fuse: add io-uring buffer pool and zero-copy documentation
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) module, specifically covering its usage of io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy techniques. The new documentation explains how buffer pools decouple the relationship between entries and payload buffers, allowing for more efficient memory usage and enabling optimizations such as incremental buffer consumption. It also describes how to use this feature by setting a flag during registration and providing the payload buffer pool through an iovec payload in the first submission queue element (sqe). The documentation includes a high-level overview of how fuse manages the buffer pool internally, including how buffers are selected, copied, and recycled.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series adds buffer pools and zero-copy to the fuse io-uring implementation, allowing servers to pass in a managed buffer pool that reduces memory usage requirements and enables future optimizations. The decoupling of ents and buffers also eliminates the need for dedicated payload buffers, reducing memory waste. Additionally, the series introduces zero-copy functionality, which allows direct access to client pages or page cache folios without copying data through an intermediary buffer, requiring CAP_SYS_ADMIN privileges and registered buffers.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: The author simplified the logic for fetching and sending off the next request by removing duplicated code from fuse_uring_send() and decoupling request fetching from the send operation, making control flow clearer and reducing unnecessary parameter passing.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern about the copy_to_user() logic in fuse_uring_out_header_has_err(), fuse_uring_args_to_ring(), and fuse_uring_copy_to_ring(). They moved this logic into a new function, copy_header_to_ring(), to make it more clear and centralize error handling. The changes are already implemented in the updated patch.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern about the copy_from_user() logic being scattered throughout the code by extracting it into a new function called copy_header_from_ring(). This makes the logic more clear and centralizes error handling and rate-limited logging.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern about the header copying interface by introducing an enum to identify which part of the header needs to be copied, improving the interface and simplifying both kernel-space and user-space header addresses copying.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: Author addressed a concern about payload copying logic, added a new helper function setup_fuse_copy_state() to encapsulate the copy state setup for payload copying, and modified existing code to use this new function.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: Author addressed a concern about magic indices in the iovec array by replacing them with named constants FUSE_URING_IOV_HEADERS and FUSE_URING_IOV_PAYLOAD, making the code self-documenting and preparing for buffer ring support.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern about the tight coupling between ents and buffers in fuse io-uring, explaining that they have decoupled them by allowing servers to pass in a buffer pool (a contiguous allocation of buffers) that the kernel will use for servicing ents/requests. The buffer ring logic is used to manage the buffer pool, selecting and recycling buffers on demand. The author provided detailed explanations of how the buffer pool is managed and how it reduces memory usage requirements.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern about the handling of registered buffers in the buffer pool setup code, explaining that the pages backing the buffers are pinned upfront when registering buffers with io-uring, and that this eliminates the overhead of pinning/unpinning user pages. The author also clarified the requirements for using registered buffers, including setting the FUSE_URING_REGISTERED_BUFFERS init flag during registration and following standard protocol for using registered buffers.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the zero-copy implementation, specifically the need for CAP_SYS_ADMIN and FUSE_URING_REGISTERED_BUFFERS flags to enable zero-copy. The author explains that these flags are necessary because zero-copy allows the server direct access to the client's underlying pages, rather than operating on an intermediary buffer. They also provide detailed information about the request flow for both the write and read paths, including how page-backed and non-page-backed args are handled.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledged that the zero-copy patch has a dependency on Jens's io-uring tree patches, which may not be mergeable in time for 7.2. She asked Miklos to decide how to sync between the two trees and suggested co-shipping the bufpool and registered buffer patches with the zero-copy patch.
review_comments.raw_body:
Miklos,

The zero-copy patch has a dependency on the bvec patches in Jens's
io-uring tree. Since the merge window opens Monday, it's probably too
tight now to cross-coordinate this for 7.2. What do you prefer for
syncing between the two trees? If Jens stages the patches in a stable
branch, are you fine with pulling that in and then applying the fuse
patches on top?

Patches 7 and 8 (bufpools and registered buffers) don't have this
dependency, but to make the uapi cleaner under one single umbrella
flag, it would be better to co-ship them with the zero-copy patch.

Patches 1-6 are refactoring changes that have been out since October
and have value on their own, independent of the bufpool and zero-copy
work. Could you please pick these up for 7.2 so they don't have to be
carried and repeatedly rebased?

Thanks,
Joanne

review_comments.author: Jens Axboe
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Jens Axboe restaged the io_uring prep patches in his for-7.2/io_uring-fuse branch and suggested they can be merged either in the current merge window or carried forward if fuse isn't ready yet, noting potential conflicts with other branches.
review_comments.raw_body:
FWIW, I reviewed the patches again and they still look fine to me. So
the 4 io_uring prep patches are staged, they are in my
for-7.2/io_uring-fuse branch. They can either go in for this coming
merge window, or we can carry them forward if fuse isn't quite ready
yet.

Since there will be conflicts with either the io_uring branches or block
branches, the above branch is for-7.2/io_uring + for-7.2/block to avoid
that.

-- 
Jens Axboe

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v7 0/4] io_uring: extend bvec registration

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 1/4] io_uring/rsrc: rename io_buffer_register_bvec()/io_buffer_unregister_bvec()
series_items.patch_summary: This patch renames two io_uring APIs: `io_buffer_register_bvec()` and `io_buffer_unregister_bvec()`. The first is renamed to `io_buffer_register_request()` to prepare for supporting kernel-populated buffers in fuse io-uring, which will register bvecs directly. The second is renamed to `io_buffer_unregister()` to make its name more generic, as it will be used by both the new and old APIs for unregistration. This change is part of a series of patches that aim to support registering bvecs directly.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 2/4] io_uring/rsrc: split io_buffer_register_request() logic
series_items.patch_summary: This patch splits the initialization logic in io_buffer_register_request() into a helper function called io_kernel_buffer_init(). This is done to prepare for supporting kernel-populated buffers in fuse io-uring, which will reuse this logic. The new helper function takes several parameters and initializes an io_mapped_ubuf structure, while the main function remains largely unchanged. This change allows for more modular code and sets the stage for future enhancements.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 3/4] io_uring/rsrc: add io_buffer_register_bvec()
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function, io_buffer_register_bvec(), which allows registering an array of bio vectors (bvecs) to be used in the io_uring context. This is a preparatory step for implementing zero-copy support over fuse using io_uring. The function takes several parameters, including the bvec array, its length, and flags for issue and release operations. It initializes an io_mapped_ubuf structure and sets up the bio vectors within it. If successful, it returns 0; otherwise, it returns a negative error code.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 4/4] io_uring/rsrc: rename and export IO_IMU_DEST / IO_IMU_SOURCE
series_items.patch_summary: This patch renames and exports two io_uring flags, IO_IMU_DEST and IO_IMU_SOURCE, to more descriptive names IO_BUF_DEST and IO_BUF_SOURCE. This change is necessary for the io_buffer_register_bvec() path, which requires the buffer to be both readable and writable. The patch updates several files, including include/linux/io_uring_types.h, io_uring/io_uring.c, io_uring/rsrc.c, and io_uring/rsrc.h, to reflect these changes.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series refactors and extends the io_uring registered buffers infrastructure to allow external subsystems to register pre-existing bvec arrays directly, aiming to make fuse zero-copy possible.
